commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=94b82225ddb33b99c4dc645793e30b4640ecb7c0)
commands.review_summary:
The branch keys the Skills tab list rendering with Lit `repeat(skill.skillKey)` and adds a jsdom regression test for checkbox state after a skill leaves the filtered tab.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +54. Total +59 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-backed: current main reuses unkeyed Lit child parts by list position while the checkbox property can be changed by the user before `skills.update` reloads the filtered report. I did not run a browser repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for after-fix real UI proof; the code follow-up is limited to tightening the new test.

target.title: fix(ui/skills): prevent skill toggle state transfer on list mutation (#89661)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89661.

When a skill toggle was flipped and the skill moved to another tab (e.g., from Disabled to Ready), the checkbox UI state was incorrectly transferred to the next skill in the list. This happened because the skill list in `renderSkills` used a plain `.map()` without keys, causing lit-html to reuse DOM nodes by position rather than by identity.

## Changes

- `ui/src/ui/views/skills.ts`: Replaced plain `.map()` with lit's `repeat` directive, using `skill.skillKey` as the unique key. This ensures each skill gets its own stable DOM node, preventing state leakage between list items.
- `ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts`: Added a regression test that renders two disabled skills, simulates one being enabled (removed from the disabled tab), and asserts that the remaining skill's toggle stays unchecked.

## Verification

- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts` — all 5 tests pass (including the new regression test).
